content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 17, 1999

Dorothea J. Moore
144 West 27th Street
Merced, California 95340

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-99-051

Dear Ms. Moore:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your request does not pertain to a specific governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.² Please note that this letter should not be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place. (Section 18329(b)(8)(A).)

QUESTION

Does the Act restrict you from managing a project relating to the city hall building?

CONCLUSION

As a former member of both the city council and the redevelopment commission, the revolving door provisions of the Act do not apply to you.

FACTS

On September 21, 1998, you resigned from the Merced City Council and the Redevelopment Commission. You accepted a position as a consultant for A.T.D. Micro, Inc., for the period of November 1-30, 1998, until a formal contract could be developed. As of

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-99-051
Page 2

December 1, 1998, you became a full-time employee for ATD.³ Your duties include managing a
project partially funded by the redevelopment commission.

ANALYSIS

The Act's "revolving door" provisions, which prohibit a former public official from
taking part in certain proceedings in which the official participated while in government service
and which prohibit a former public official from appearing before or communicating with his or
her former agency for one year, apply only to "state administrative officials" or to designated
employees of "state administrative agencies." (Sections 87400-87406.)

In your capacity as a city council member and a member of the redevelopment
commission, you were a public official of two local governmental agencies. Therefore, the
revolving door provisions of the Act do not apply to you. However, you may wish to consult
with the City of Merced to ascertain if the city has a local ordinance regarding revolving door
provisions for former employees.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division

SGC:LD:tls

___________________________

³ Other facts included in your request for advice relate to past conduct. We do not provide advice
regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation I 8329(c)(3).)
